taskDescription: Arrange the four sub-triangles of the anterior triangle from superior to inferior as they appear anatomically.
paragraphs: Submental triangle: bounded by the anterior bellies of both digastric muscles and the hyoid bone; contains submental lymph nodes draining the tip of the tongue and floor of the mouth.
Digastric (submandibular) triangle: bounded by the mandible above and both bellies of digastric below; contains the submandibular gland, facial artery, and hypoglossal nerve.
Carotid triangle: bounded by SCM, posterior belly of digastric, and superior belly of omohyoid; contains the carotid bifurcation, internal jugular vein, and cranial nerves IX-XII.
Muscular triangle: bounded by SCM, superior belly of omohyoid, and midline; contains the infrahyoid strap muscles, thyroid gland, and trachea.
